page: Amazon Web Services m5d.xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 Intel Xeon 8259CL processor operating at 3.1 GHz, configured with 4 dedicated vCPUs across 2 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The CPU includes a 35 MB L3 cache and supports instruction sets such as AVX, AVX-512F, AES, and FMA. The instance is equipped with 16.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core.

A key feature of this instance is its bundled local storage, consisting of a 150 GB NVMe SSD instance store volume. It provides a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps. In benchmark evaluations, the m5d.xlarge demonstrates modest compute capabilities, with stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU performance falling into the bottom 25% tier. Memory bandwidth tests for large blocks also rank in the bottom 25%, though PassMark memory scores reach the middle 50% average tier.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the m5d.xlarge offers a balanced resource profile for general-purpose workloads that require low-latency local scratch storage without the overhead of network-attached storage. Given its benchmark profile, which includes weak performance in database operations, Redis SET operations, and static web serving, this instance is best suited for development environments, light build systems, or applications requiring temporary local caching rather than high-throughput production databases.
description: Amazon Web Services m5d.xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor with 4 dedicated vCPUs, 2 physical cores, and a 3.1 GHz clock speed. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, it includes 16.0 GB of DDR4 memory and a bundled 150 GB NVMe SSD local instance store. While its compute and memory bandwidth benchmarks generally fall into the bottom 25% tier, its PassMark memory score achieves an average rating. With a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps, this instance is designed for workloads that benefit from a balanced CPU-to-memory ratio and low-latency local storage. It is best suited for general-purpose applications, software development, and workloads requiring temporary local caching. The instance provides a reliable platform for non-intensive tasks that require fast local disk access.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m5d.xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 4 vCPUs, 16 GB of DDR4 RAM, and a 150 GB local NVMe SSD, designed for development and basic caching workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m5d.xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 4 vCPUs, 16 GB of DDR4 RAM, and a 150 GB local NVMe SSD.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose instance featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processing and low-latency local NVMe SSD storage for development workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8259CL processor with 4 dedicated vCPUs and 3.1 GHz clock speed
16.0 GB of DDR4 memory providing a balanced 4:1 RAM-to-vCPU ratio
Bundled 150 GB NVMe SSD local instance store for low-latency scratch space
1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor
Best suited for general-purpose applications, development environments, and local caching
